channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Calls On Private Companies to Take On a Bigger Role in Cyber
item.description: In his National Cybersecurity Strategy, President Trump sought an expanded role for private firms in cyberwarfare. He did not take on China or Russia in the document.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump on Friday released the National Cybersecurity Strategy, which calls for private companies to play a more active role.

item.title: State Department Bypasses Congress to Send Israel More Than 20,000 Bombs
item.description: The sale had been under informal review in Congress, but the State Department declared an emergency because of the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Multiple explosions near Mehrabad airport amid US-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Friday as part of their joint war on Iran.

item.title: Trump Maintains That U.S. Has Ample Weapons as Iran War Depletes Stocks
item.description: After meeting with defense contractors at the White House, President Trump said the companies were all committed to increasing production.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Farah Stockman and Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said an increase in weapons production would come “as rapidly as possible” to “the highest levels of quantity.”

item.title: Justice Dept. Denounces Federal Judges in Fight Against Law Firms
item.description: The Trump administration had signaled earlier this week that it was ready to abandon four executive orders seeking to punish law firms, but abruptly reversed course the next day.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: Wilmer Cutler Pickering Hale and Dorr LLP
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Justice Department is accusing federal judges of undermining President Trump’s authority.

item.title: Vulnerable California Republican Darrell Issa Drops Re-election Bid
item.description: The decisions by Darrell Issa and Kevin Kiley, made newly vulnerable by redistricting, demonstrated the challenges Republicans face in a difficult midterm environment.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Darrell Issa has served for 12 terms in the House.

item.title: Trump Echoes F.D.R. and Grant in Calling for ‘Unconditional Surrender’
item.description: President Trump demanded that Iran capitulate in the war with the United States, invoking a phrase made famous by statesmen and generals.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump declared on Friday that he would settle for nothing short of “unconditional surrender” by Iran.

item.title: Brandon Herrera’s YouTube Gives Democrats More Hope in West Texas Race
item.description: What had been a safe G.O.P. seat was looking more attainable for Democrats after Representative Tony Gonzales bowed out in favor of a hard-right candidate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Brenda BazáN/Associated Press
item.media:description: Brandon Herrera had forced a run-off with the incumbent, the Republican Tony Gonzales, before Mr. Gonzales ended his re-election bid.

item.title: Kristi Noem Survived Many Crises. Then She Crossed a Trump Red Line.
item.description: President Trump, who values loyalty, has at times tried to distance himself from his administration’s own actions when they become politically toxic.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Kristi Noem testifying before the Senate Judiciary Committee on Tuesday, where her comments led the president to fire her later in the week.

item.title: Iran War Poses Test for Justice Dept. After Firings Deplete National Security Ranks
item.description: Firings, resignations and diversions to the president’s priorities have left elite counterterrorism and counterintelligence units stretched thin, current and former officials say.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Glenn Thrush
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Directives from the Trump administration have led to an exodus of experienced investigators and prosecutors.

item.title: Russia Is Sharing Intelligence With Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The information has included satellite imagery showing the locations of military personnel. But some officials played down the significance of the partnership.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Pavel Bednyakov
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia has spoken to his Iranian counterpart to discuss the U.S.-Israel attack.

item.title: Talarico’s Social Media Paper Trail, the Jobs Report and More Politics News
item.description: For some Democrats, generational change comes with a cringey social media past.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Talarico, the state representative from Texas who is now the Democratic nominee for Senate in the state.

item.title: Divisive F.D.A. Vaccine Regulator Is Resigning
item.description: Dr. Vinay Prasad drew criticism for overriding career scientists and rejecting drugs by companies seeking agency approval.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: U.S. Food and Drug Administration
item.media:description: Dr. Vinay Prasad’s refusal to accept Moderna’s application for a new mRNA flu vaccine, prompting an uproar among companies and experts.

item.title: Florida Bar Retreats From Statement Saying Lindsey Halligan Was Under Scrutiny
item.description: The Florida bar said that it had “erroneously” made that assertion, disclosed in a letter last month, and that no investigation into Ms. Halligan was pending.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Jacquelyn Martin/Associated Press
item.media:description: A spokeswoman for the bar declined to comment on whether she had been contacted by administration officials.

item.title: How The Times Tracks Down the Connections Behind Trump’s Pardons
item.description: Reporters tapped sources, combed through public records and scrutinized social media to penetrate the web of influence and money underlying the president’s clemency grants.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Over the course of his first term and the first year of his second, President Trump has granted pardons or commutations to at least nine inmates who served at Otisville’s camp or the adjacent medium-security prison.

item.title: As Trump Focuses on Cuba, DOJ Seeks Charges Against Its Leaders
item.description: The order could set the stage for a drive for regime change in Cuba, a goal President Trump has expressed.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Alan Feuer, Luke Broadwater and Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jason A. Reding Quiñones, the United States attorney for the Southern District of Florida, is said to be looking to bring criminal charges against Cuba’s leaders.

item.title: Trump Regulatory Czar Jeffrey Clark Leaves White House
item.description: Jeffrey B. Clark oversaw the dismantlement of government restrictions on greenhouse gas emissions and water and air pollution. He had also faced a criminal probe in connection with the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jose Luis Magana/Associated Press
item.media:description: Jeffrey B. Clark speaking during the Conservative Political Action Conference in Oxon Hill, Md., in 2024.

item.title: Jeremy Larner, Who Wrote ‘The Candidate,’ a Political Film Classic, Dies at 88
item.description: His Oscar-winning 1972 screenplay starred Robert Redford as an idealistic public interest lawyer making a run for the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Top National Symphony Leader Quits in New Blow to Kennedy Center
item.description: The executive director, Jean Davidson, said her departure reflects frustration at the turmoil that has engulfed the arts center.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: “It’s no secret that this has been a really hard year,” the executive director of the National Symphony Orchestra, Jean Davidson, said of her decision to leave her post after less than three years.

item.title: Why a G.O.P. War Powers Hawk Was a No on Reining Trump In on Iran
item.description: Many Republicans deferred almost unquestioningly to President Trump after he began a sweeping military offensive in the Middle East. Senator Todd Young was more conflicted, but ended up in the same place.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Todd Young, Republican of Indiana, at the Capitol on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Demands ‘Unconditional Surrender’ by Iran, Shifting U.S. Objectives Again
item.description: President Trump laid out yet another, more ambitious goal of U.S. military action, one that could extend the war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians mourning the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader, in Tehran on Thursday night.

item.title: With Iran, Trump Takes the U.S. to War Without the Public’s Support
item.description: In opening a military campaign against Iran, President Trump is the first president in modern times to take the United States to war without the backing of the public.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump on the USS George H.W. Bush in October. Polling shows public support for striking Iran is below what his predecessors enjoyed initially when they used force overseas.

item.title: Lawmaker Asks Court to Block Trump From Closing Kennedy Center
item.description: The president has said he plans to shut down the center for two years starting this summer for a “complete rebuilding.”
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “President Trump’s own words indicate that he is planning to demolish the existing Kennedy Center building,” says a lawsuit by Representative Joyce Beatty, Democrat of Ohio.

item.title: From 2006: Coretta Scott King, a Civil Rights Icon, Dies at 78
item.description: First known as the wife of the Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., she later became an avid proselytizer for his vision of peace and nonviolent social change.
item.dc:creator: Peter Applebome
item.media:credit: Ric Feld/Associated Press
item.media:description: Coretta Scott King in 1986.

item.title: From 1994: Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis Dies of Cancer at 64
item.description: She became an object of fascination to generations of Americans after her husband, President John F. Kennedy, was assassinated in 1963.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden

item.title: From 1962: Mrs. Roosevelt Dies at 78 After Illness of Six Weeks
item.description: The death of the former first lady, a noted humanitarian and an active force in the Democratic Party, was mourned by people around the world.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Bettmann, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Eleanor Roosevelt at the 1956 Democratic National Convention in Chicago.

item.title: Defense Without U.S. Help Is a Live Topic for Canada, Japan and Australia
item.description: The leaders of Japan and Canada are making a unified front on defense cooperation as President Trump raises the pressure over military spending.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving in Tokyo on Friday as part of a tour that also included Australia.

item.title: Trump Remarks on U.S.S. Cole and Iran Raise Questions in Guantánamo Case
item.description: Prosecutors have offered up no evidence supporting a link that the president claimed between Iran and the attack in Yemen in 2000, defense lawyers say.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: U.S. Navy/Getty Images
item.media:description: The bombing of the U.S.S. Cole off the coast of Yemen was seen as a precursor of the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.

item.title: Potomac River in D.C. Is Safe, Officials Say. But Locals Still Worry About the Poop.
item.description: A broken pipe sent a gusher of sewage into the river near Washington. Some people may try to row, sail or fish. But skeptics are steering clear.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin and Salwan Georges
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: The collapse of a major sewer line sent 243 million gallons of raw human waste flowing into the Potomac. Here, a pipe reroutes wastewater.

item.title: Georgia Candidate Colton Moore Copies Marjorie Taylor Greene Playbook
item.description: Colton Moore, who hopes to win a special election on Tuesday to replace Ms. Greene, has the same flair for the dramatic and the ultra-MAGA persona, but he lacks one thing: a Trump endorsement.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset
item.category: Georgia Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election)
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: Other politicians tend to “hide under the desk,” said Colton Moore who is running in Tuesday’s special election for Georgia’s 14th Congressional District. He does not.

item.title: The Apartments at the Center of Mamdani’s Affordable Housing Crisis
item.description: The mayor’s call to “freeze the rent” galvanized the 69 percent of New Yorkers who don’t own their homes. But the city’s landlords claim the math doesn’t add up.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mahler
item.media:credit: Gail Albert Halaban for The New York Times
item.media:description: Crotona Park East, the Bronx

item.title: Pardon Industry Offers Rich Offenders a Path to Trump
item.description: One inmate paid lobbyists and lawyers with ties to the president’s team and walked free. Others are following his blueprint, but it is not always clear who can deliver.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
